description: To provide collaborative research opportunities for qualified non-immigrant alien scientists who hold a doctoral degree or its equivalent in one of the biomedical or behavioral sciences.

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Write a self-contained, 30-line maximum abstract summarizing the Specific Aims, Research Strategy (methods), and anticipated results. Maintain a professional, accessible tone suitable for public release. Explicitly state the central hypothesis and the significance of the findings. Crucially, ensure no Personal Identifiable Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) is included, as this content will be made public via RePORTER upon funding. Adhere strictly to NIH formatting guidelines for PDF attachments.
guidelines: The content will be made available to the public through the NIH Research Portfolio Online Reporting Tool (RePORTER) if the application is funded.
Must summarize the project's aims, methodology, and expected outcomes.
Do not include Personal Identifiable Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in the abstract.
search_queries: NIH project summary abstract requirements F series
Example NIH abstract structure aims methodology
Formatting guidelines NIH 30 line abstract
PII PHI exclusion NIH grant application
NIH RePORTER public abstract guidelines
grant_sections.title: Project Summary/Abstract

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Write a concise, non-technical statement (2-3 sentences typically) explaining how the proposed research addresses a critical public health problem or advances public health knowledge. Clearly articulate the connection between the scientific aims and the ultimate benefit to human health or disease prevention. Avoid jargon and technical details. This statement must be suitable for public release via RePORTER; strictly exclude all PII and PHI.
guidelines: The content will be made available to the public through RePORTER if the application is funded.
Must clearly articulate how the proposed research relates to public health needs.
Do not include Personal Identifiable Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI).
search_queries: NIH public health relevance statement examples
Translational research impact statement NIH
How to write public health relevance for F grant
NIH RePORTER public health relevance guidelines
NIH relevance statement length
grant_sections.title: Public Health Relevance Statement

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Develop a comprehensive narrative covering Specific Aims (1 page) and the Research Strategy (Significance, Innovation, Approach). The Approach section must detail experimental design, methods, data analysis plans, and include preliminary data supporting feasibility. Emphasize rigor, reproducibility, and statistical justification for all proposed experiments. Maintain a formal, scientific tone. Ensure strict adherence to the FOA's page limits and NIH formatting standards (e.g., font size, margins). Do not include sensitive PII or PHI.
guidelines: Must include Specific Aims and a detailed Research Strategy.
Ensure the narrative adheres to page limits specified in the Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA).
Follow standard NIH formatting requirements for attachments (fonts, margins, headers/footers).
Do not include sensitive PII or PHI in the narrative.
search_queries: NIH Research Strategy structure Significance Innovation Approach
Specific Aims page limit NIH F series
Rigor and reproducibility guidelines NIH grant
Statistical analysis plan requirements NIH research
Preliminary data integration in NIH grant application
Addressing anticipated pitfalls and alternative strategies NIH
NIH formatting requirements PDF attachments
NIH F series Research Plan page limits
grant_sections.title: Research Plan Narrative (e.g., Specific Aims, Strategy)
topics: Justification of sample size and statistical methods
Assessment of potential risks and mitigation

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Construct a detailed training plan that clearly links the proposed research project to the applicant's long-term career goals (e.g., becoming an independent investigator). Specify didactic coursework, practical research training, and professional development activities (e.g., grant writing, presentation skills). Describe the frequency and nature of interaction with the sponsor/co-sponsor. Include a dedicated section on the plan for instruction in the Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR), detailing subject matter, format, frequency, and duration.
guidelines: This narrative is mandatory for Fellowship Awards (F series).
Must detail the applicant's proposed training activities and career development goals.
Adhere to NIH formatting requirements for PDF attachments.
search_queries: NIH F series training plan requirements
Responsible Conduct of Research instruction NIH fellowship
Career development goals for NIH F award
Mentorship plan structure NIH fellowship application
Didactic training components NIH F series
grant_sections.title: Training Plan Narrative (Required for Fellowship Awards)

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Complete the PHS Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Information form (F.500) comprehensively. If a clinical trial is involved, detail the study design (e.g., phase, randomization, blinding), primary outcomes, and statistical analysis plan. For all HSR, describe the risks, benefits, and procedures for protecting participants, including the informed consent process and IRB status. Strictly ensure that no sensitive PII or PHI is entered into any part of this form or associated attachments.
guidelines: Complete the PHS Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Information form (F.500).
Must detail all aspects related to the involvement of human subjects or clinical trials.
Ensure strict handling of data by not including sensitive PII or PHI.
search_queries: NIH PHS Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Information F.500
Definition of NIH clinical trial
Data Safety Monitoring Plan requirements NIH HSR
Inclusion of women and minorities NIH policy
Informed consent process NIH grant application
grant_sections.title: Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Information (PHS F.500)

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Develop a concise, two-page maximum plan detailing the management and sharing of scientific data in compliance with the NIH DMS Policy. Specify the data types, formats, and metadata standards that will be used. Identify the chosen repository (e.g., NCBI, institutional repository) and the timeline for data sharing (no later than publication). Clearly outline the roles and responsibilities for DMS activities and confirm that the budget justification includes associated costs. Address privacy, confidentiality, and any restrictions on sharing.
guidelines: Must describe how scientific data will be managed and shared.
The plan must adhere to NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy requirements.
Specify the types of data to be shared and the timelines for sharing.
search_queries: NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy requirements
Selecting NIH approved data repository
Budgeting for NIH DMS plan costs
Scientific data definition NIH grant
NIH DMS plan template
grant_sections.title: Data Management and Sharing (DMS) Plan
topics: Standards for data collection and metadata

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Detail the plan for making unique research resources (e.g., cell lines, antibodies, novel software, animal models) available to the broader scientific community. If the project generates large-scale human genomic data, include a specific statement confirming compliance with the NIH Genomic Data Sharing (GDS) Policy. Specify the mechanism (e.g., through a repository, MTA) and timeline for resource distribution. If no unique resources are generated, state this explicitly and briefly explain why the standard DMS plan is sufficient.
guidelines: Describe the plan for sharing research resources, such as unique reagents or model organisms.
If applicable, include plans for sharing genomic data (GDS Policy).
Ensure compliance with NIH policies regarding resource availability.
search_queries: NIH resource sharing plan requirements
Genomic Data Sharing Policy NIH compliance
Sharing unique research reagents NIH
Material Transfer Agreement guidelines NIH
NIH model organism sharing policy
grant_sections.title: Resource Sharing Plan(s)

grant_sections.generation_instructions: The sponsor must provide a statement detailing their qualifications, previous experience mentoring trainees (especially NIH fellows), and their commitment to the applicant's training plan. The statement must explicitly describe the research environment, institutional resources, and financial support available to the fellow. It should outline the frequency and nature of supervision and the process for evaluating the fellow's progress, ensuring the environment is conducive to the fellow's success.
guidelines: This statement is mandatory for Fellowship Awards.
Must detail the sponsor's commitment to the applicant's training and research.
The statement should describe the resources and environment available to the fellow.
search_queries: NIH F series sponsor statement requirements
Example sponsor statement NIH fellowship
Sponsor mentoring history NIH grant
Institutional resources description for NIH fellow
Sponsor evaluation plan for F awardee
grant_sections.title: Sponsor/Co-Sponsor Statements (Required for Fellowship Awards)
topics: Plan for trainee supervision and evaluation

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Provide a clear, itemized justification for every requested cost, linking expenditures directly to the Specific Aims and methodology described in the Research Plan. If using a detailed budget, justify the percentage of effort (FTE) for all personnel. If using a modular budget, justify the overall module amount and any significant variations. Ensure costs for Data Management and Sharing (DMS) are included and justified. Maintain consistency with institutional salary caps and NIH policy.
guidelines: Justify all requested costs clearly and specifically.
Distinguish between modular and detailed budget requirements based on the FOA.
Explain the necessity of personnel, equipment, and supply costs relative to the project aims.
search_queries: NIH detailed budget justification requirements
Modular budget justification NIH guidelines
Justifying personnel effort FTE NIH grant
Allowable costs NIH fellowship budget
Budgeting for publication costs NIH
grant_sections.title: Budget Justification

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Describe the physical and intellectual environment where the research will be conducted. Detail the available laboratory space, major equipment, and access to institutional core facilities (e.g., sequencing, imaging). Explicitly state how the environment is conducive to both the proposed research and the applicant's training goals, demonstrating strong institutional support for the fellowship. Focus on resources essential for the project's success and the fellow's development.
guidelines: Describe the facilities available for the proposed research.
Detail the institutional support and resources provided to the project team.
Explain how the research environment is suitable for the proposed work and training.

topics: Adequacy of laboratory and office space
Availability of specialized equipment and core facilities
Suitability for proposed research and training
